title: Timing might be harder to model than direction in trading AI
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
One thing I find interesting when thinking about AI in trading:

Direction is relatively straightforward to model (trend, momentum, etc.).

But timing is much harder.

Because even if a setup is “correct”:

* entering too early changes the risk profile
* waiting too long reduces the edge
* and confirmation isn’t always clearly defined

So the question becomes:

👉 Can AI realistically model *when* to act, not just *what* to do?

Especially when timing often depends on:

* micro-structure
* context shifts
* or subtle changes in price behavior

Curious to hear from people working on trading systems:

👉 Do you treat timing as part of the model, or as a separate layer?
